Q:V301. Boris Johnson wants to change the Withdrawal Agreement made between Theresa May’s government and the European Union. If Boris Johnson is unable to make changes to the Withdrawal Agreement that enables it to pass the House of Commons before October 31st, which of the following should he do?
column13: Go ahead with Brexit on October 31st even if it means leaving with ‘no deal’
column14: Delay Brexit until we can negotiate a deal that can pass the House of Commons
column15: Cancel Brexit and decide to remain in the European Union after all

column10: Q:V304. Boris Johnson has asked the Queen to call a new session of parliament, which requires “proroguing” parliament. Parliament was already due to be on holiday during conference season in late September, but the time that Parliament isn’t sitting has now been extended and all unfinished legislation before the prorogation on Monday has fallen. Opposition MPs oppose the prorogation because they want to maximise the time available to stop a no-deal Brexit.
